## Voxis Competitive Analysis — Real-Time Voice Translation Landscape

Scope: 20 analyzed GitHub repos spanning live S2S translators, research models, caption/ASR infra, and batch dubbing tools. Voxis = Windows desktop, native-simultaneous (Gemini `gemini-3.5-live-translate-preview`), driverless WASAPI loopback, music-preserving dubbing, dual SaaS+BYOK distribution.

---

### 1. TL;DR

- **Voxis owns the hard product problem nobody else solves end-to-end**: driverless system-audio capture + music-preserving dubbing + native-simultaneous + bot-free + two-way meeting. The closest live competitors (`phuc-nt/my-translator`, `sokuji`) are mic-only or require VB-CABLE; the strong tech (`hibiki`, `StreamSpeech`, `seamless_communication`) are research models with no capture/mixing/product layer.
- **Three gaps are repeated by nearly every competitor and are now table-stakes**: (1) **subtitle/SRT/VTT + transcript export**, (2) **the full 70+ language list** (vs Voxis's ~12), and (3) a **pluggable/offline backend** to kill the single-vendor Gemini-preview supply risk. These show up in 18 of 20 repos.
- **The single biggest structural threat is supply risk**, not a competitor: Voxis is welded to one Google *preview* model. `hibiki` (open weights, CC-BY, voice transfer, ICML'25) and `seamless_communication`/`StreamSpeech` are credible drop-in offline backends that would convert that liability into an "offline/private" feature.
- **The cheapest, highest-leverage wins are pure UI/config**: exposing 70+ languages and emitting SRT/VTT from the input/output transcription data Gemini *already returns on the wire* (confirmed by Google's own `gemini-live-api-examples`). Both are "low effort / high impact" and close the most-cited gaps with near-zero engineering.
- **Latency is model-bound (~3s) and you can't shrink it — so compete on *perceived* latency**: live dual-column captions (so users *see* the translation forming before they hear it) and Twilio's "forward original audio first" gap-filler are the two ship-now moves that neutralize the latency narrative.

---

### 2. Landscape table

| Repo | Stars | What it is | Closest to Voxis on | Their edge |

| `kizuna-ai-lab/sokuji` | ~910 | Electron + MV3 extension live S2S | Meeting/virtual-mic injection | 7 providers + full offline (sherpa-onnx/WebGPU), browser extension, 99+/55+/53 langs, voice cloning |
| `ictnlp/StreamSpeech` | ~1.3k | ACL'24 unified streaming S2ST model | Native-simultaneous claim | Client-tunable latency (chunk size), fully local, intermediate text, published CVSS-C BLEU |
| `kyutai-labs/hibiki` | ~1.5k | Open-weights simultaneous S2S model | Native-simultaneous + voice | Open weights (CC-BY), voice transfer, on-device (MLX/Rust/iOS), ICML'25 benchmarks |
| `facebookresearch/seamless_communication` | 11.8k | FAIR S2ST model family | Simultaneous policy / breadth | ~100 langs, EMMA tunable latency, PRETSSEL prosody preservation, SimulEval harness |
| `QuentinFuxa/WhisperLiveKit` | ~10.5k | Self-hosted real-time STT infra | (Captions, not S2S) | Diarization, pluggable ASR, SRT/VTT, OpenAI+Deepgram APIs, browser ext, cross-platform |
| `niedev/RTranslator` | ~10.2k | Offline on-device Android translator | Live conversation translation | 100% offline (Whisper+NLLB ONNX), documented quantization, multi-party BLE |

| `Softcatala/open-dubbing` | ~408 | Apache CLI offline dubbing | (Batch dub) | Demucs stem separation, pyannote diarization, gender-matched voices, post-edit JSON loop |

| `Kedreamix/Linly-Dubbing` | ~3.3k | Batch dub + lip-sync (dormant) | (Batch dub) | Demucs/UVR separation, voice cloning, diarization, digital-human lip-sync |

| `cyberofficial/Synthalingua` | ~401 | Self-hosted caption tool | (Captions only) | Offline Whisper, HLS/Twitch ingest, OBS overlay, Discord webhook, demucs |

| `twilio-samples/live-translation-openai-realtime-api` | 125 | Twilio Flex two-way phone translation | Two-session Meeting topology | OpenAI Realtime backend, per-direction prompts, "forward audio first" mask, latency telemetry |

| `ufal/whisper_streaming` | 3.6k | Seminal streaming-Whisper library | Streaming policy (reference) | LocalAgreement-2 tunable latency knob, offline, 99+ langs, WER benchmark modes |

---

### 3. Where Voxis is BEHIND (gaps), grouped

**Languages (only ~12 exposed vs model's 70+)** — Solved by virtually everyone: `sokuji` (99+/55+/53), `seamless_communication` (~100), `WhisperLiveKit`/`WhisperLive`/`whisper_streaming` (99+), `KrillinAI`/`pyvideotrans` (100+), `RTranslator` (33+40). Google's own `gemini-live-api-examples` proves arbitrary `target_language_code` works with `echo_target_language=True` and **no extra config** — this is purely a UI list that gates capability the model already has.

**Speaker diarization (none)** — A repeatedly-cited moat-gap. Solved by `WhisperLive` & `WhisperLiveKit` (pyannote/Sortformer online clustering), `pyvideotrans`/`Linly-Dubbing` (WhisperX), `open-dubbing` (pyannote + gender→voice). Critical for multi-party Meeting mode where one flat voice is confusing.

**Subtitle/transcript export (none)** — The most universal gap. SRT/VTT/word-timestamps shipped by `WhisperLiveKit`, `WhisperLive`, `Synthalingua`, `KrillinAI` (4 SRT variants), `pyvideotrans`, `LiveCaptions-Translator` (CSV), `Hearsay` (timestamped MD). `hibiki`/`StreamSpeech` emit timestamped text natively. **The data already exists on the Voxis wire** — Gemini Live returns `input_audio_transcription` + `output_audio_transcription` (confirmed by `gemini-live-api-examples` and `twilio` sample). This is serialization + a download button.

**Offline / pluggable backend (none — single Gemini preview)** — The biggest structural risk. Offline stacks proven on commodity hardware by `RTranslator` (Whisper+NLLB ONNX on a phone), `Synthalingua`/`Hearsay`/`whisper_streaming` (faster-whisper local), `sokuji` (sherpa-onnx WebGPU), and open-weights `hibiki`/`StreamSpeech`/`seamless_communication`. Pluggable provider seams in `my-translator` (4 engines), `sokuji` (7), `LiveCaptions-Translator` (10+), `WhisperLiveKit` (`--backend auto`). No fallback if the preview model is deprecated/repriced/throttled.

**Platforms (Windows-only)** — Cross-platform shipped by `my-translator`, `sokuji`, `KrillinAI`, `pyvideotrans` (win/mac/linux); `hibiki`/`seamless` add iOS/Apple-Silicon; `RTranslator` is Android. Voxis's WASAPI/pycaw/IPolicyConfigVista COM lock-in blocks a port. A browser extension (`sokuji`, `WhisperLiveKit`, `WhisperLive`) is the cheaper cross-platform on-ramp.

**Voice/TTS quality (fixed Gemini voices)** — Voice cloning / speaker-timbre transfer in `hibiki` (CFG-steered voice transfer), `sokuji` (Palabra/Doubao), `KrillinAI`/`pyvideotrans`/`Linly-Dubbing` (CosyVoice/XTTS/GPT-SoVITS), `seamless` (PRETSSEL prosody), `kensonhui` (speaker embeddings). Voxis output is a generic voice with no speaker identity carryover.

**Audio separation quality** — Voxis's M/S center-suppression *attenuates* center-panned dialogue but leaks on mono/off-center content and can't *remove* dialogue. True source separation (Demucs/UVR) in `open-dubbing`, `pyvideotrans`, `Linly-Dubbing`, `Synthalingua` cleanly splits vocal vs music stems — a stronger version of Voxis's signature moat (heavy/batch-only, not real-time).

**UX & ecosystem** — No client-tunable latency knob (`StreamSpeech` chunk size, `whisper_streaming` LocalAgreement-n, `seamless` EMMA threshold expose one). No quality/WER/latency benchmark (`hibiki`, `seamless`, `StreamSpeech`, `whisper_streaming`, `twilio` all publish/instrument). No CLI/programmatic API (`KrillinAI` manifest+agent skills, `WhisperLive`/`WhisperLiveKit` OpenAI/Deepgram-compatible). No file/URL ingestion (`gemini-live-api-examples` CLI, `KrillinAI`, `pyvideotrans`). No glossary/hotwords (`my-translator` custom terms, `WhisperLive` hotwords). Broken auto-update (internal known gap).

---

### 4. What competitors do BETTER (techniques worth respecting)

- **Perceived-latency masking** — `twilio` plays the *original* audio to the other party before the translation lands (`FORWARD_AUDIO_BEFORE_TRANSLATION`), eliminating dead air. The single best lever against a fixed latency floor.
- **Live confirmed-prefix captions** — `whisper_streaming`'s LocalAgreement-2 commits a partial transcript only after 2 overlapping decodes agree → stable, non-flickering captions. Combine with `StreamSpeech`/`hibiki` dual-column source|target streaming so users *see* translation forming.
- **One-session-per-language multiplexing** — `gemini-live-api-examples` `TranslationSessionManager` ref-counts subscribers, tears down idle sessions via `sendBeacon` — the cost-control pattern for any future broadcast/webinar mode.
- **True stem separation over M/S ducking** — `open-dubbing`/`Linly-Dubbing` use Demucs Hybrid Transformer to reconstruct a clean music bed, solving Voxis's mono/off-center leak case (batch-only due to compute).
- **Documented quantization** — `RTranslator`'s INT8 + KV-cache + encoder/decoder weight-dedup (NLLB 2.5GB→1.3GB, 8s→2s) is a reusable blueprint for any future desktop-local path.
- **Latency instrumentation** — `twilio` measures `vad_speech_stopped_time → first_audio_buffer_add_time` per call; `whisper_streaming` separates model-latency from compute-latency (`--comp_unaware`). Turns "~3s" into a defensible measured number.
- **Per-direction editable prompts with fidelity guardrails** — `twilio` (`AI_PROMPT_CALLER/AGENT`: "no additions/omissions/alterations") and `mohammed-bahumaish` (context-window history into MT). A cheap quality/glossary/formality knob.
- **Auto hardware-tier onboarding** — `Hearsay` first-run wizard probes VRAM and picks model/precision; enterprise silent-install flags for RMM/SCCM/Intune.
- **ASR hygiene** — `Synthalingua` auto-blocklist (phrase blocked 3×/10min) + repetition suppression kills Whisper hallucination loops; `Hearsay` filler-word strip + duplicate collapse for clean transcripts.

---

Even transcript-only labels add value. pyannote/Sortformer online clustering. |

Heavy models → start in export/Meeting-outgoing path. Consent/ethics gating. |

---

### 6. Where Voxis already WINS (defend the moat honestly)

- **The combination is the moat, and no one replicates it.** Driverless WASAPI process-exclude loopback (never re-translates its own TTS) + music-preserving dubbing + native-simultaneous + bot-free + two-way Meeting + BYOK/zero-telemetry exists nowhere else as one shippable product. Every strong competitor wins on *one slice* and leaves the rest unbuilt.
- **System-audio capture is a genuine capability gap, not polish.** `sokuji`, `kensonhui`, `mohammed-bahumaish` capture *microphone only* — they cannot translate Netflix/YouTube/game audio without the user wiring loopback. `Synthalingua` needs stereo-mix/virtual cable. Voxis's core use case is structurally unavailable to them.
- **Driverless beats VB-CABLE.** `sokuji` still ships `vb-cable-installer.js`; `kensonhui`/`mohammed-bahumaish` hard-require BlackHole/VB-Cable. Voxis removed exactly that friction via pycaw session ducking + IPolicyConfigVista endpoint switching.
- **Native-simultaneous is architecturally real.** The strong open models (`hibiki`, `StreamSpeech`, `seamless`) match it — but they're research models with no capture/mixing/product. The *product* competitors (`sokuji`, `WhisperLiveKit`, `Synthalingua`, `RTranslator`, `LiveCaptions-Translator`, `twilio`) are staged ASR→MT→TTS or turn-based, inherently laggier and choppier on continuous speech.
- **Full output audio DSP chain.** M/S center-suppression DubbingDucker (RBJ peaking-cut @2.3kHz) + DelayLine RTT-alignment + LookaheadLimiter + place_center is a real-time mixing layer the caption tools (`WhisperLive`, `WhisperLiveKit`, `Synthalingua`, `LiveCaptions-Translator`) have *zero* analog for (they emit no audio), and even the research models output raw speech with no ambient mixing.
- **Any-to-any 70+ targets.** `whisper_streaming`/`WhisperLive`/`WhisperLiveKit`/`Synthalingua` Whisper-translate is **English-output only**; `kensonhui` is English-only; `StreamSpeech`/`hibiki`/RTranslator-local are narrow pairs. Voxis's target breadth ceiling far exceeds the offline/local field.
- **Real commercial spine.** Signed SaaS .exe + PocketBase auth + Stripe billing + usage heartbeat *and* a Fernet-encrypted zero-telemetry BYOK OSS build. Most competitors are BYOK-only, AGPL-restricted (`sokuji`, `Synthalingua`), GPL (`KrillinAI`, `pyvideotrans`), non-commercial-weights (`seamless`), or unlicensed (`gemini-live-api-examples`, `kensonhui`) — legally blocked from a paid product.
- **No GPU required on the client.** `StreamSpeech`/`seamless`/`hibiki`/`WhisperLive` need CUDA/A100-class hardware for real-time; Voxis runs on commodity Windows by offloading to cloud.

---

### 7. Strategic recommendation

Make four moves, in order. **First, harvest the free wins this quarter**: expose the 70+ languages and ship SRT/VTT + searchable transcript export — both are pure UI/serialization against data Gemini already returns (proven by Google's own `gemini-live-api-examples`), and they close the three most-cited gaps in the entire field at near-zero engineering cost while feeding SEO/marketing. **Second, win the latency narrative you can't win on physics**: add live dual-column source|target captions (so users *see* the translation forming, masking the ~3s floor) plus Twilio's "forward original audio first" gap-filler and a measured "X.Xs behind" telemetry badge — perception is the only latency lever left and these are med-effort/high-impact. **Third, retire the single biggest structural risk**: define a pluggable backend seam behind `LiveTranslator` now (cheap insurance), wire OpenAI Realtime as a second backend using Twilio's `AudioInterceptor.ts` as the reference (identical PCM framing), then add an offline faster-whisper/`hibiki`-1B tier — this converts the Gemini-preview supply liability into an "offline/private/zero-cost" enterprise SKU and BYOK developer feature that *no* live-S2S product currently offers with Voxis's capture+dubbing chain. **Fourth, deepen the moat where you're already ahead**: ship speaker diarization (the most-cited gap) to make multi-party Meeting mode genuinely differentiated, and reserve voice-cloning + Demucs stem separation for a future batch/"Studio" export path where their latency cost doesn't threaten the live loop. Do *not* chase cross-platform desktop ports (a rewrite against your WASAPI/COM moat) — if you need reach beyond Windows, a Chromium extension is the cheaper on-ramp.


---

# Voxis Competitive Analysis — Addendum (Newly-Scanned Repos)

This addendum covers eight repos the first scan missed. Net finding: **none compete with Voxis's actual product** (driverless, native-simultaneous, music-preserving voice-to-voice dubbing). But collectively they commoditize the three capabilities Voxis lists as gaps — diarization, transcript/subtitle export, and pluggable/offline backends — and one of them (`recallai/real-time-translator-demo`) is the cleanest foil for sharpening Voxis's bot-free moat.

## 1. Addendum Landscape

| Repo | Stars | What it is | Why it matters to Voxis | Their edge over Voxis |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **thainph/node-trans** | ~125 | Electron live-caption app; driverless native loopback (WASAPI/ScreenCaptureKit/PulseAudio) → STT → translated **text** overlay | Closest architectural cousin: driverless-loopback + overlay thesis, but cross-platform and with the pluggable/offline backend Voxis lacks | mac/Win/Linux capture; pluggable cloud (Soniox) **or** offline (faster-whisper + Ollama/LibreTranslate); pyannote 3.1 diarization; Markdown export. **No LICENSE = legally all-rights-reserved** |
| **Dadangdut33/Speech-Translate** | ~655 | Tkinter realtime+batch Whisper STT → MT subtitle generator | Independently solved driverless WASAPI loopback (pyaudiowpatch); covers Voxis's export gap fully | 6-format export (.srt/.vtt/.ass/.tsv/.json/.txt) w/ word timestamps (stable-ts); batch file import; multi-engine MT; offline. **Frozen since Jan 2024** |
| **SamirPaulb/real-time-voice-translator** (LinguaSync) | ~410 | Tkinter mic-only S2S via free Google APIs (recognize_google → deep-translator → gTTS) | Reference for cheap cloud S2S + tri-platform packaging; markets "tone/emotion" but ships none (verified in main.py) | Win/Linux/macOS installers (cx-Freeze); zero-key/zero-cost; full Google-Translate language matrix; transliteration. **Dormant since late 2023** |
| **botbahlul/crx-live-translate** | ~118 | MV3 Chrome/Edge extension overlaying translated subtitles on tab video | Stakes out the Chromium-extension + "translate any tab" surface Voxis has zero presence in | Browser-native distribution; in-tab caption + fullscreen overlay; cross-platform via browser; zero-key. **Unpublished, fragile (needs CORS-Unblock + unofficial Google endpoint), stale Jul 2024** |
| **digitask-dev/teamsTranscribe** | 0 | PyQt prototype: faster-whisper captions for Teams/Zoom, bot-free | Reference for the floating always-on-top caption overlay surface Voxis lacks | Local/offline STT; live PyQt overlay; user-selectable model tiers (tiny→large-v3). **Transcribe-only, no translation; pre-alpha** |
| **FluidInference/FluidAudio** | 2.3k | Production Swift/CoreML on-device audio-AI SDK (ASR + diarization + VAD + TTS) | Hard evidence that **streaming, on-device diarization is commoditized** — the exact capability Voxis lists as a moat | LS-EEND streaming diarizer (10 speakers, 20.7% DER @500ms, 100ms frames); published WER/DER/RTFx benchmarks; ANE acceleration; pluggable model zoo. **Apple-only, no translation** |
| **pasrom/meeting-transcriber** | 61 | macOS Core-Audio-tap meeting notes app; local diarize + LLM Markdown protocol | Reference impl for Voxis's three biggest gaps at once: diarization, export, pluggable backend | Dual-track capture; cross-session speaker identity (voice embeddings + cosine); pluggable ASR (WhisperKit/Parakeet) + any OpenAI-compatible LLM. **macOS-only, batch, no translation; audio-tap silence bug #79** |
| **davidamacey/OpenTranscribe** | 68 | Self-hosted GPU batch media platform (WhisperX + PyAnnote v4 + microservices) | Architectural inverse: an export/diarization/search "inspiration mine" for an offline layer | PyAnnote v4 diarization + boundary correction; cross-media voice fingerprinting; full export (SRT/VTT/TXT/JSON + burned-in); hybrid search; LLM summaries. **Batch-only, Docker+GPU heavy** |
| **KoljaB/RealtimeSTT** | ~9.9k | Low-latency streaming STT **library** (~12 pluggable backends) | The de-facto STT infra layer competitors assemble translators on; accepts the exact 16kHz mono PCM16 Voxis already emits | Two-pass WebRTC→Silero VAD; ~12 swappable offline backends; stabilized-vs-interim callbacks; syllable-boundary scheduling (~81.6% fewer ASR calls); FastAPI multi-user server. **STT-only, no translation/diarization/export** |
| **recallai/real-time-translator-demo** | ~24 | Sample over Recall.ai's bot-joins-the-call interpreter API | The canonical **inverse of Voxis's bot-free moat** — sharpens positioning | Bot joins any platform server-side (OS-agnostic); native diarization; 7 swappable STT providers; captions visible to all participants. **Text-only, bot/consent friction, paid per bot-minute** |

## 2. node-trans Deep-Dive

`thainph/node-trans` is the single most instructive repo here because it executes Voxis's own thesis — **driverless native system-audio loopback feeding a real-time translation overlay** — but does so cross-platform (Windows WASAPI via a C#/.NET 8 `audiocap.exe`, macOS ScreenCaptureKit via a Swift binary, Linux PulseAudio/PipeWire) and with the pluggable/offline backend Voxis structurally lacks (cloud Soniox **or** a fully air-gapped faster-whisper + Ollama/LibreTranslate stack, plus pyannote 3.1 diarization and Markdown export). It stops at translated *text* — no TTS, no ducking, no music preservation, no meeting/virtual-mic path — so Voxis still owns the voice category outright. The concrete lessons to steal: (a) its **decoupled native-capture binary boundary** (`audiocap` emits raw PCM s16le to stdout, normalized to 16kHz mono and chunked to fixed 120ms/3,840-byte frames) is the cleanest seam Voxis has for a future macOS/Linux port without rewriting the engine; (b) its **lazy-loaded STT factory + base64-PCM-over-newline-delimited-JSON worker protocol** is a copyable template for a pluggable backend; (c) its **diarize→whisper automatic fallback on timeout/crash** and typed error channels are a proven resilience pattern. Caveat: it ships **no LICENSE file**, so it is legally all-rights-reserved — study the design, do not vendor the code.

## 3. Diarization Is Being Commoditized

Voxis's roadmap treats speaker diarization as a future moat. **FluidAudio (2.3k stars), OpenTranscribe, and meeting-transcriber together prove it is becoming table-stakes, not a moat.** FluidAudio ships *streaming* diarization on consumer Apple hardware — LS-EEND, end-to-end, no clustering, up to 10 speakers at 20.7% DER on a 500ms step (74.5x RTFx, 100ms frame updates) — alongside two more diarizers (Sortformer, offline Pyannote). OpenTranscribe runs PyAnnote v4 with a boundary-correction pass (~32% word-speaker-error reduction) scaling to 20–50+ speakers **plus** cross-media voice fingerprinting (label propagation across files). meeting-transcriber adds *persistent cross-session speaker identity* via stored voice embeddings + cosine similarity, on macOS, as an indie hobby project. The strategic implication: Voxis should ship diarization as a **fast-follow checkbox, not a headline moat**, and aim past plain labels — its real differentiation is what these text-only tools structurally cannot do: **map distinct diarized speakers to distinct TTS playback voices in the dubbed output** (and in Meeting mode it already has two distinct Live sessions to label cheaply). On Windows the implementation path is an ONNX-exported streaming diarizer (pyannote/Sortformer) feeding the existing `_GatedSource` 16kHz frames.

## 4. New / Sharpened STEAL-THIS Rows

| Idea | Source repo(s) | Effort | Impact | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SRT/VTT/JSON transcript export from the existing Gemini Live text stream | Speech-Translate, OpenTranscribe, recallai, crx-live-translate | low | high | Highest-leverage gap-closer; **no new ML** — transcript already flows through the engine. Universally demanded across every captioning competitor |
| Pluggable translation/STT backend behind `LiveTranslator` (Gemini default + local faster-whisper/Parakeet fallback) | node-trans, RealtimeSTT, FluidAudio, OpenTranscribe, meeting-transcriber, Speech-Translate | high | high | Retires the single-`gemini-3.5-live-translate-preview` supply risk. RealtimeSTT's `use_microphone=False`/`feed_audio` PCM contract + node-trans's base64-PCM-over-JSON worker are proven, copyable seams |
| Streaming speaker diarization → per-speaker **TTS voices** (not just labels) | FluidAudio (LS-EEND), OpenTranscribe (PyAnnote v4), meeting-transcriber | high | high | Sharper than first scan: aim past text labels to *distinct dubbing voices per speaker* — novel vs every text-only competitor. Ship ONNX pyannote/Sortformer on Windows |
| Decoupled native-capture binary boundary (PCM-over-stdout/named-pipe) separating COM/WASAPI core from the Python engine | node-trans (`audiocap`) | med | med | The natural seam for a future macOS (ScreenCaptureKit) / Linux (PulseAudio) port without rewriting the engine per-OS |
| Always-on-top caption overlay rendering live translated text alongside the dubbed audio | teamsTranscribe (PyQt), crx-live-translate, recallai, node-trans | low–med | med–high | Reuses existing transcript events into a new pywebview/WebView2 always-on-top window; serves deaf/HoH + noisy environments |
| WebRTC VAD pre-gate in front of Silero (two-pass VAD) | RealtimeSTT | low | med | Cheap WebRTC first-pass cuts Silero calls + sharpens endpointing in `_GatedSource` — fewer false speech-starts = fewer billed minutes on the Saver/gated preset |
| Expose all 70+ model languages instead of ~12 | node-trans, Speech-Translate, LinguaSync, crx-live-translate | low | high | Pure config/UI/i18n change; model already does the work. Competitors' "breadth" edge evaporates the moment Voxis lists the full set |
| Cross-session speaker identity via persisted voice embeddings + cosine similarity | meeting-transcriber, OpenTranscribe | med | med | "Speaker 2" becomes a recurring named person across meetings — B2B differentiator once basic diarization exists |
| Per-track capture (mic vs system on separate channels through the pipeline) | meeting-transcriber (dual-track) | med | med | Cleaner speaker separation + structurally removes self-TTS bleed; complements process-exclude loopback and boosts any future diarization |
| Offline file-import / batch dub mode (drop a video → dubbed track + subtitles) | Speech-Translate, OpenTranscribe, node-trans | high | med | Extends Voxis from live-only into the content-localization market; reuses the translation core but pulls toward batch — scope carefully |
| Optional post-session LLM summary (BLUF / action items) over the saved transcript (BYO endpoint) | meeting-transcriber, OpenTranscribe, recallai | low–med | med | Cheap, high-margin SaaS upsell once a transcript exists; matches Otter/Granola/Fireflies expectations |
| Two-tier transcript stream (fast interim + "stabilized" settled text) | RealtimeSTT, node-trans (partial/utterance) | med | med | Lower perceived latency in the transcript panel; stabilized segments map directly to subtitle cues for SRT/VTT |
| Thin Voxis SDK/CLI (capture + translate frames in/out) for embedding (OBS, automation) | FluidAudio, RealtimeSTT | med | med | Opens streamer/accessibility integration surfaces the app-only packaging blocks; fits OSS BYOK positioning |
| Publish a WER/BLEU/latency benchmark harness with hardware-attributed numbers | FluidAudio (Benchmarks.md) | med | med | Closes the no-WER-benchmark gap; reproducible numbers detect Gemini-preview regressions and become an SEO/marketing credibility asset |

## 5. Positioning Contrast: Bot-Free vs Bot-Based

`recallai/real-time-translator-demo` is the textbook **bot-based** interpreter — a cloud virtual participant *joins* the Zoom/Meet/Teams/Webex call, transcribes server-side, and overlays translated **text** captions back as a screenshare visible to the whole room. That makes it OS-agnostic and out-of-the-box diarized, but it carries the structural costs Voxis was built to avoid: a visible third-party participant, mandatory consent/recording friction (a hard blocker in legal/medical/confidential and personal settings), text-only output (it never *speaks* the translation back), and a metered per-bot-minute cloud bill. Voxis's **driverless, bot-free, process-exclude WASAPI loopback + spoken 24kHz playback + zero-telemetry BYOK** is the exact inverse — nobody joins the call, nothing is recorded in a third-party cloud, the translation is *heard* not read, and it works on any system audio (video, games, any app), not just meetings a bot can join. That contrast — "no bot joins your call, nothing is recorded in our cloud, and you hear the translation" — is a genuine compliance/privacy moat Recall structurally cannot match; name it explicitly in positioning.

---

## Appendix — Completeness Critic (missing repos / under-explored angles)

I have enough verified data to deliver the completeness critique.

## Completeness Critique — Voxis GitHub Competitive Scan

### (a) Notable confirmed repos NOT covered (verified to exist)

The 20 covered repos lean heavily on the dubbing / Whisper-streaming / SDK clusters but miss several direct product-equivalents — especially the closest one:

- **thainph/node-trans** — https://github.com/thainph/node-trans (~125 stars). THE closest uncovered competitor. Electron desktop app, **Windows WASAPI loopback (no virtual driver)** + macOS ScreenCaptureKit + Linux, always-on-top overlay, cloud (Soniox) **or local (Ollama/LibreTranslate)** translation. This is essentially Voxis's driverless-loopback + overlay thesis in cross-platform Electron form, with the offline/pluggable-backend story Voxis lacks. Its omission is the biggest gap in the scan.

- **Dadangdut33/Speech-Translate** — https://github.com/Dadangdut33/Speech-Translate (~655 stars). Mature Python app, Whisper ASR + free/LibreTranslate MT, **records speaker output on Win8+**, mic+speaker, batch + live, Win/mac/Linux. High-star direct overlap on the "translate what your PC is playing" use case.

- **SamirPaulb/real-time-voice-translator** (LinguaSync) — https://github.com/SamirPaulb/real-time-voice-translator (~410 stars). Cross-platform speech-to-speech GUI; mic-only (not loopback) but high visibility and markets "preserves tone/emotion."

- **botbahlul/crx-live-translate** — https://github.com/botbahlul/crx-live-translate (~118 stars). Chrome/Edge extension, live caption/subtitle translation of any tab audio. Directly addresses the **"no Chromium extension" Voxis gap** — worth covering as the category-defining open example. (Same author's `js-live-audio-video-translate` is the web-template variant.)

- **digitask-dev/teamsTranscribe** — Python + faster-whisper + system-audio capture + **PyQt floating overlay** aimed at Teams/Zoom/browser calls. The bot-free meeting-overlay pattern Voxis competes on; verify stars (appears small/new).

- **FluidInference/FluidAudio** — https://github.com/FluidInference/FluidAudio (~2.3k stars). Note: NOT translation (ASR + diarization + VAD + TTS, Apple-only). Belongs in the scan only as evidence of **strong open-source streaming diarization on-device** — i.e. it commoditizes the diarization moat Voxis is counting on. Same for **pasrom/meeting-transcriber** (macOS, bot-free auto-record Teams/Zoom/Webex, local diarization) and **davidamacey/OpenTranscribe** (PyAnnote v4 + translation, self-hosted).

Lower priority but real: **KoljaB/RealtimeSTT** (the streaming-STT library many of these build on — infrastructure layer worth one line) and **oddlama/whisper-overlay** (Wayland push-to-talk overlay).

### (b) Under-explored search angles

1. **Browser-extension category entirely absent** from the 20 covered — yet it's a named Voxis gap and has multiple live OSS examples (crx-live-translate, xignoe/videoTranslatorExtenstion, Gemini-Live tab-dubber extensions). This is the most under-explored category.
2. **Local/offline + pluggable-backend tier** (Ollama/LibreTranslate MT, BlackHole/Voicemeeter routing) — node-trans, Speech-Translate, loop-rogue/realtime-audio-transcriber all hit the exact "single-backend / cloud-only" weakness Voxis admits. Scan is light here.
3. **macOS-native real-time stack** (ScreenCaptureKit capture + CoreML/ANE diarization: FluidAudio, meeting-transcriber) — directly attacks the "Windows-only" and "no diarization" gaps and is barely represented.
4. **OpenAI gpt-realtime-translate / Realtime-API simultaneous-interpreter** projects beyond the two Twilio/Gemini samples covered (e.g., event-interpreter apps surfaced in search) — the direct architectural rival to Gemini-Live native-simultaneous.
5. **Bot-based meeting interpreters** (Recall.ai-style bot that joins the call) — the inverse of Voxis's "bot-free" moat; covering 1-2 would sharpen the positioning contrast.

### (c) Fabricated / mislabeled

No covered repo appears fabricated — the 20 owner/name pairs are all plausible and consistent with known projects; the cluster (StreamSpeech, hibiki, seamless_communication, WhisperLive, whisper_streaming, pyvideotrans, KrillinAI, RTranslator, etc.) is real. **One caution:** "VoxGo" surfaced repeatedly in search as a gamer-overlay loopback-translation tool but I could **not** confirm a concrete repo URL/owner — do NOT add it without verification (likely a marketing-page name, not a GitHub repo). Also confirm `teamsTranscribe`'s exact owner slug before citing.
